weather.sun.spring.text: The spring sun warms the millet shoots.
A kind sun. The earth remembers how to breathe.

weather.sun.summer.text: The summer sun beats the field flat.
The sun hammers the earth, and the earth takes it.

weather.sun.autumn.text: The sun slants. The grain is ready.

weather.rain.spring.text: A spring rain dampens the millet.
Rain. The mud grabs at ankles.

weather.rain.autumn.text: The rain does not care that the grain is in.

weather.snow.winter.text: White on white. The world has no edges today.

weather.frost.winter.text: Frost scrawls on the paper windows.
The ground rings under the heel.

weather.wind.dry.text: The dry wind carries dust across the plain.
A yellow wind, tasting of old harvests.

weather.wind.cold.text: The north wind finds every seam.
A wind that remembers the steppes.

weather.drought.heavy.text: The earth cracks like old pottery.
No rain. No rain. No rain.
The wells give back less each day.

weather.drought.breaking.text: Clouds gather in the west. The old men watch.
A hush on the fields, as if the sky is listening.

weather.overcast.calm.text: A grey sky, patient and still.
Clouds like old cloth, low and close.

terrain.yellow_plains.millet_field.text: The field. Brown stalks, brown earth.
Millet to the edge of sight.

terrain.yellow_plains.wheat_field.text: The wheat bends in long slow waves.
A gold country, waiting for the sickle.

terrain.yellow_plains.road.text: The road, packed dirt, bends east toward the river.
The road. It is always the road.
Cart-ruts deep as a child's arm.

terrain.yellow_plains.crossroads.text: Four roads meet under a crooked ash.
The crossroads. Someone has left an offering.
Where roads cross, ghosts keep watch.

terrain.yellow_plains.river_low.text: The river runs thin. Stones gleam.
The river bed shows its bones.

terrain.yellow_plains.river_flood.text: The river has taken the bank. Brown water, fast.
The fields are a sea. Roofs float like rafts.

terrain.yellow_plains.village.text: The village — ten huts, a single well, an old banyan.
Dogs bark. Smoke curls. A child is crying somewhere.
Mud walls, thatch, the smell of cooking fire.

terrain.yellow_plains.hut.text: The hut, walls of packed clay, roof of thatch.
A single lamp. A single bowl. Enough.

terrain.yellow_plains.temple_ruined.text: A broken temple, half swallowed by the hill.
The incense holder is full of rain.

terrain.yellow_plains.shrine.text: A small shrine, a new ribbon tied to the post.
The shrine keeps what it can.
A dish of millet, a cup of water, someone's prayer.

terrain.yellow_plains.hills.text: Low hills, pale grass, a hawk circling.
The hills. Nothing lives up there worth the climb.
The hills roll like a sleeping animal.

terrain.yellow_plains.burial_mound.text: A burial mound, grass high, stones sunken.
Names on the stones, worn smooth by wind.

terrain.yellow_plains.inn_yard.text: The inn yard, straw, a tethered mule.

terrain.yellow_plains.well.text: The well, its ropes frayed, its bucket dented.
Water rises slow out of the stone.
The well. Cold breath from below.

time.dawn.cold.text: A cold dawn, the sky the colour of a knife.
The first light is a grey blade at the horizon.

time.dawn.mist.text: Mist on the fields, thinning as the light climbs.
The mist holds the night a little longer.

time.noon.work.text: Noon. The sun straight overhead, the work unfinished.
The bell at the village. No one looks up.

time.noon.rest.text: The noon bell. Shade under the banyan. A shared bowl.
The men eat standing, watching the weather.

time.dusk.village.text: Dusk settles on the village. Lamps come on one by one.
Smoke rising straight. A good sign.

time.dusk.field.text: The fields go blue in the dusk. The birds settle.
The last light lies long across the furrows.

time.night.quiet.text: A quiet so deep the blood hears itself.

time.night.watchful.text: Night, and someone somewhere is awake with a blade.
Night. A dog will not stop barking.
The lamps put out early. No one wants to be seen.

npc.bandit.threat.text: The bandit shows his teeth more than his blade.
Three of them. One is laughing. That is the worst one.

npc.bandit.flee.text: The bandits break and run. Their shouting fades.
They run. So does the breath.
One drops his knife in the dust. No one picks it up.

npc.merchant.haggle.text: The merchant weighs his words like copper.
'Two coppers less,' he says. 'And I ate this morning.'
He names his price. Then a smaller one. Then a sigh.

npc.merchant.story.text: The merchant tells of a city where the river is made of stone.
He has been south. He has been north. He remembers prices.
'A man asked me once,' he says, 'what a life is worth. I told him: less than salt.'

npc.monk.blessing.text: The monk touches the forehead. 'Go quietly,' he says.
He hands over a grain of rice. 'Enough,' he says.

npc.monk.warning.text: 'Do not walk the east road tonight,' the monk says.
The monk will not say what he saw.
'Some debts,' he says, 'are paid in bone.'

npc.elder.wisdom.text: The old woman says: 'The field that eats you feeds you too.'
'Patience,' the elder says. 'The year is long.'
She laughs, once, dry as leaves. 'I was your age when the river took my brother.'

npc.elder.warning.text: 'Don't take from that well,' the old man says. 'Not ever.'
He shakes his head. 'You are too young to know.'
'I have seen this weather before,' he says. 'It does not end well.'

npc.traveler.stranger.text: A stranger at the fire. Muddy boots. Tired eyes.
The stranger nods. Says nothing. Drinks.
He has come from far. His cloak smells of another weather.

npc.traveler.thank.text: The traveller bows low. 'A debt,' she says. 'I will remember.'
A bow. A promise. A name asked for, given.
She leaves a copper in the rice jar before she goes.

npc.child.laugh.text: A child runs by, laughing at nothing the adults can see.
Two children chase a paper kite across the stubble.

npc.child.cry.text: A child cries somewhere in the village, on and on.
A child crying. No one comes.
The child is too young to say what hurts. It only hurts.

emotion.hunger.mild.text: A small emptiness in the belly, nothing new.
$[CHARACTER] tightens the sash and keeps walking.

emotion.hunger.severe.text: A cold gnawing beneath the ribs. Everything smells of food.
$[CHARACTER] thinks of grass, of bark, of the cat.

emotion.fear.sudden.text: A shape at the edge of the light. The heart knocks.

emotion.fear.long.text: A fear that has lived here a long time, like a mouse in the thatch.
Fear that has become quiet. The worst kind.
$[CHARACTER] has learned to sleep with one eye toward the door.

emotion.hope.small.text: A small hope, no bigger than a sparrow.
$[CHARACTER] catches a good smell and keeps it a moment.

emotion.hope.dawn.text: The kind of hope that comes with first light.
$[CHARACTER] smiles, and is surprised by it.

emotion.resignation.tired.text: A tiredness that goes past the bone.
The shoulders bow. The world does not notice.

emotion.resignation.old.text: The long patience of people who have buried more than they have named.
$[CHARACTER] nods once. This is how it goes.
The sigh of a person who stopped arguing with the sky.

emotion.anger.flash.text: A hot anger, sudden as a snapped stalk.
$[CHARACTER] clenches the jaw so hard it aches.
A word rises and is bitten back.

emotion.anger.cold.text: A cold anger, set and patient.
$[CHARACTER] does not speak. The hand is steady.
An anger that has learned to wait.

emotion.grief.fresh.text: Grief like a new cut, still surprised by itself.

emotion.grief.faded.text: An old grief, smooth as river-stone.
$[CHARACTER] remembers the name, and the year, and the rain.
Grief that has found its place and settled.

emotion.wonder.quiet.text: A moment when the world looks unlikely and beautiful.
The light does something to the wheat. It is almost too much.

emotion.wonder.sudden.text: A heron lifts out of the reeds like a piece of paper taking flight.
Something moves in the grass — a fox, a spirit, a trick of dusk.

action.work.plowing.text: The plough bites. The ox leans. The furrow opens.
Hands on the plough. Mud to the ankle.
Row by row, the field is turned.

action.work.harvest.text: Sickle swings, stalks fall. The arms burn.
The threshing floor fills, sheaf by sheaf.

action.rest.sleep.text: Sleep takes $[CHARACTER] before the lamp is out.
The straw is warm. The dark is kind.
Sleep. Without dreams, if the gods are merciful.

action.rest.meditation.text: $[CHARACTER] sits. The breath slows. The world keeps a small distance.
Legs folded. Eyes half-lidded. The mind at low tide.
Stillness. Only the pulse in the ears.

action.flee.desperate.text: Run. Do not look. Do not think. Run.
The legs go before the mind catches up.

action.fight.desperate.text: A blow. A cry. Another blow.
No plan, only teeth and hands and the wish to live.

action.travel.slow.text: $[CHARACTER] walks the long road, one step for each thought.
Slow walking. The pack is heavy but known.
The miles pass. The sky barely moves.

action.travel.urgent.text: $[CHARACTER] pushes on past dark. The lantern swings.
Fast walk. Faster. The breath ragged.
The road unwinds. Every bend matters.

action.eat.simple.text: Millet porridge. Salt. A strip of pickled radish.
$[CHARACTER] eats slowly, as if thanking each spoonful.
A warm bowl. A cold night. Enough.

action.drink.water.text: Cold water from the well, iron-tasting, good.
Water. A small blessing, counted twice.

reflection.mood.sorrow.1.text: The heart makes its small, slow room for grief.
$[CHARACTER] does not weep. It is past weeping.
Sorrow settles the way dust settles — everywhere, in time.

reflection.mood.sorrow.2.text: Some losses are a weather that never quite clears.
$[CHARACTER] carries a name the way one carries an old stone.
Grief is not a door. It is a house one learns to live in.

reflection.mood.rage.1.text: $[CHARACTER] wants to break something, anything, the sky if possible.
The anger finds no door. It lives under the skin.
A rage that tastes of iron.

reflection.mood.serenity.1.text: For a breath, $[CHARACTER] is exactly where $[CHARACTER] is.
A quietness settles. Nothing missing. Nothing needed.
The world is simply itself, and that is enough.

reflection.mood.serenity.2.text: Breath in. Breath out. The fields forgive their farmer.
A stillness the old monks would call a kind of wealth.
$[CHARACTER] does not reach for anything. It is enough to watch.

reflection.mood.paranoia.1.text: Someone is watching. Or no one. The difference is thin.
Every kindness has a ledger. Every ledger has a price.

reflection.mood.resolve.1.text: $[CHARACTER] sets the jaw. One thing at a time. Then the next.
The choice is made. The body will catch up.
No god will do this. Only these hands.

reflection.mood.resolve.2.text: $[CHARACTER] will walk until the walking is done.
What is owed will be paid. In full. In time.
The road narrows to a single step. That step is taken.

reflection.mood.melancholy.1.text: $[CHARACTER] watches the smoke climb and thinks of nothing in particular.
A long evening. The mind drifts like a paper boat.
The years are a procession of small weathers.

reflection.mood.melancholy.2.text: The lamps come on in the village. $[CHARACTER] is not in any of them.
Some days are a soft, grey weight, and no more.
The river goes where it goes. $[CHARACTER] stays where $[CHARACTER] is.

memory.witness.frost_palm_severing.fragm.text: A stranger's hand moved once, quick as a splinter. The air shivered where it had been.

memory.witness.frost_palm_severing.parti.text: The form is familiar. Three fingers, the pivot of the hip — a cold art that severs before the strike is named.

memory.witness.frost_palm_severing.compl.text: The Frost Palm Severing. He has seen this art completed. The lungs fill with winter; the blade has already fallen.

memory.manifest.frost_palm_severing.text: In the quiet of the hour the hand moves on its own. He watches his own fingers draw the severing line, and remembers that he never learned it.

memory.witness.crimson_spear_river.fragm.text: A thrust — too quick, too red. The spear crossed the distance before the body began to fall.

memory.witness.crimson_spear_river.parti.text: The movement is known now. Shoulder lowered, breath held, the red line cast across the throat.

memory.witness.crimson_spear_river.compl.text: The Crimson Spear. Completed form. One thrust ends where the river begins again.

memory.manifest.crimson_spear_river.text: His breath finds a rhythm he did not teach it. The spear is a river; the river has always run through him.

memory.witness.silent_waters_scripture.f.text: He found a page. The characters dissolved when he tried to pronounce them.

memory.witness.silent_waters_scripture.p.text: The scripture returns in fragments. Each line a held breath; each syllable a door that closes.

memory.witness.silent_waters_scripture.c.text: The Scripture of the Silent Waters. He knows the whole of it now, as one knows the shape of one's own skull.

memory.manifest.silent_waters_scripture.text: In the silence between thoughts he reads himself. The syllables were there all along.

memory.witness.iron_bamboo_stance.fragme.text: A villager caught a strike without moving. The blow passed through him like wind through an old fence.

memory.witness.iron_bamboo_stance.partia.text: Iron Bamboo. The weight settles through the feet, the breath rides the spine.

memory.witness.iron_bamboo_stance.comple.text: The Iron Bamboo Stance, perfected. His body has already settled into it before the thought arrives.

memory.manifest.iron_bamboo_stance.text: He bends. He does not break. The stance is ancient and his own.

memory.witness.hollow_mountain_breath.fr.text: An old monk exhaled. The cave seemed to exhale with him.

memory.witness.hollow_mountain_breath.pa.text: Hollow Mountain Breath. The lungs become slow, become vast, become a place that is not a place.

memory.witness.hollow_mountain_breath.co.text: The Hollow Mountain Breath, complete. He breathes as a mountain breathes. Nothing is hurried; nothing is missed.

memory.manifest.hollow_mountain_breath.text: The breath opens, and keeps opening. He is sudden with space.
